title: expire_stale_sessions_before(now_ts, cutoff): parameter order is swappable with no type safety
description: Scenario: a future caller writes expire_stale_sessions_before(expire_cutoff, now_ts) (args transposed). Both are i64 Unix timestamps with no type distinction. Transposing silently stores expire_cutoff as updated_at and uses now_ts as the threshold — every session ever updated before now_ts (i.e. all of them) gets expired with updated_at set to 24h ago. No error, no warning, sessions permanently mangled. The maintenance consequence: any new call site must know the semantic order from documentation alone. Suggest renaming to make order self-documenting or adding a named-parameter struct: ExpireParams { now_ts, cutoff }.
close_reason: Fixed: second param renamed from cutoff:i64 to max_age_secs:i64; cutoff computed internally; call site updated to expire_stale_sessions_before(now_ts, 24*3600)
